commit.message:
Fix CSLINT230 false positives with single-line blocks and guard clauses

Add exemptions to BlankLineAfterBlockRule:
- Skip single-line blocks (e.g., if (x) { p += 2; continue; })
- Skip guard clause if-chains (consecutive if blocks ending with return/throw/break/continue)

Update tests to verify both exemptions and negative case.

🤖 Co-Authored-By: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
harden: embed signing key, checksum sig file, verify all scripts

- Embed signing-key.pub inline in install.sh — no external file to tamper
- Remove signing-key.pub from repo (key is in the script itself)
- Add install.sh.sig to CHECKSUMS.sha256 (defense in depth)
- Add SCRIPTS_CHECKSUMS_HASH — verifies all private repo scripts
- verify_script_signature uses embedded key, no file path needed
- Updated SECURITY.md with full trust chain documentation

Trust chain: install.sh.sig → install.sh → setup.sh + SCRIPTS-CHECKSUMS.sha256 → individual scripts

fix: pipeline completo obligatorio para toda tarea de desarrollo

- Eliminar clasificación "simple" que permitía saltarse fases
- Hotfix ahora usa pipeline completo (Design puede ser más corto)
- Todas las fases son mandatorias: Specify → Design → Implement → Verify → Delivery → KG Save
- Solo research y spike tienen flujos especiales

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.44.25: Edge case regression tests (26 tests, 81 assertions)

Add test_edge_cases.js covering 6 edge case categories:
- Pinned items: scheduler/propagation skip, CPM topology-based float, violation independence
- Pass convergence: 1 topo-sorted pass sufficient for any DAG (chains, diamonds, fan-outs)
- Zero-duration: endDate=startDate, depEnd=startDate+1, documented float=1 bug (JS falsy 0)
- Float >= 0 invariant: non-negative without external deadlines, violations independent
- FF/SF float: FF alignment, SF permissiveness (float=5 in single chain), FS dominates mixed
- Load-state: _float delete/restore, stale endDate freshening in both modes

Also adds v0.44.24 backlog entry for Critical Path pill + stale endDate fix.

commit.message:
feat: VibePredictionEngine — PsiNet × Prediction Market × Etherisc fusion

Tokenized prediction markets with secondary market trading + insurance:

PsiNet Integration:
- AI agents create markets from context graphs (AgentRegistry)
- CRPC consensus resolution (multi-validator evidence + pairwise voting)
- ContextAnchor records market research + resolution evidence
- Shapley referral chains for market discovery

Tokenized Predictions (tradeable on AMM):
- YES/NO tokens as ERC-20 compatible transferable shares
- 1 YES + 1 NO = 1 ETH collateral (guaranteed solvency)
- Transfer, approve, transferFrom for secondary market trading
- Mint/burn complete sets for entering/exiting positions

Etherisc Insurance Framework:
- Risk pools: underwriters deposit ETH to cover market outcome risks
- Parametric triggers: auto-payout when market resolves to insured outcome
- Premium pricing based on current market probability (AMM price)
- Loss ratio tracking and max 5x coverage ratio per pool
- Insurance on any market outcome — "if X resolves NO, pay me"

4 market types: BINARY, SCALAR, CATEGORICAL, PERPETUAL
4 resolution methods: SINGLE_RESOLVER, CRPC_CONSENSUS, ORACLE, DAO_VOTE

fix: gráfico patrimonio — deuda $0 pre-entrega, áreas visuales, tooltip contextual

1. Deuda pre-entrega = $0: antes de la entrega no existe crédito
   hipotecario. La línea de deuda empieza en $0 y salta al valor
   del crédito al momento de la entrega.

2. Áreas semi-transparentes: roja para deuda (hasta $0), verde para
   valor propiedad. La diferencia visual entre ambas = patrimonio neto.

3. Tooltip contextual: pre-entrega muestra "pie acumulado + plusvalía
   estimada + deuda $0 (crédito aún no comienza)". Post-entrega
   muestra "valor propiedad, deuda restante, pie + amortización,
   plusvalía acumulada".

4. Línea vertical "Entrega" ya existía — se mantiene como referencia.

* fix: align commit subjects in list command's Last Commit column

Right-pad commit ages to a uniform width so commit subjects start at
the same column regardless of age string length (e.g. "3h" vs "5mo").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: renamed Head to Changes

* feat(list): add --unit flag to show line-level diff counts

Add a --unit parameter to git-worktree-list that controls the unit of
measurement for diff counts. Defaults to "files" (current behavior).
When set to "lines", computes insertions/deletions via git diff --numstat
for the Base, Changes, and Remote columns. A spinner is shown during
the more expensive line-level computation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(list): rename --unit to --stat with summary/lines values

The "files" value was misleading since Base and Remote columns show
commit counts, not file counts. Renamed to --stat with "summary"
(default, mixed commit/file counts) and "lines" (line-level diffs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(list): add -b, -r, -a flags to show branches without worktrees

Add flags mirroring `git branch` to show branches beyond active worktrees:
  -b/--branches: local branches without a worktree
  -r/--remotes: remote tracking branches without a local branch or worktree
  -a/--all: both (equivalent to -b -r)

Non-worktree branches are displayed with dim styling and blank Path/Changes
columns. JSON output includes a "kind" field (worktree/branch/remote) and
nullable "path". Default behavior (no flags) is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(list): add daft.list.stat config for default statistics mode

Allow users to set the default --stat mode via git config
(daft.list.stat = summary|lines) instead of passing --stat lines
every time. CLI flag overrides the config value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(daemon): fix TOCTOU race in mark_our_write

The mark_our_write function was reading the current file content to
compute a checksum, but this is wrong because we want to mark the
content we're ABOUT to write. Changed signature to take content as
parameter, eliminating the race condition.

commit.message:
Enhance window detection: blue channel, directional edges, sill detection

Three improvements to the CV detection pipeline:
1. Blue channel analysis — sky detection via blue ratio scoring
2. Directional Sobel edges — horizontal edges score window frame/sill,
   vertical edges score window sides (vs. omnidirectional before)
3. Three-pass detection: coarse scan → fine refinement → sill extension
   (scans below candidate for strong horizontal edge to find sill)

Also adds brightness uniformity scoring (7 factors total, up from 5).
Tested on hard case: off-center window with competing bright shelves
and wall noise — position error <3% on all dimensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve broken Composio Outlook tool execution across entire backend

Root cause: Two systemic issues breaking ALL Composio Outlook operations.

1. Composio SDK v1.0.0-rc2 removed the `dangerously_skip_version_check`
   parameter from `Tools.execute()`, causing TypeError on every call.

2. Composio platform renamed Outlook tools with double prefix
   (e.g. OUTLOOK_GET_MESSAGE → OUTLOOK_OUTLOOK_GET_MESSAGE), causing
   404 on version resolution and KeyError on schema lookup.

Fix in oauth.py:
- Add _TOOL_NAME_MAP translating 19 legacy tool names to new platform names
- Pre-load tool schemas before execute() to prevent KeyError on _custom_tools
- Remove unsupported dangerously_skip_version_check kwarg from tools.execute()

Fix in drafts.py (original-email endpoint):
- Rewrite _fetch_full_email_body() with 3 fallback strategies:
  single message → thread fetch → draft context extraction
- Use resilient _execute_composio path with auth failover
- Auto-fetch when snippet is NULL, not just when ?full=true
- Strip JSON-breaking control characters from HTML email bodies
- Add detailed traceback logging for debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix video replies: post as text (Popcorn returns HLS, not MP4)

Root cause: Popcorn's videoUrl is an HLS manifest (.m3u8), which Twitter's
media upload API rejects (requires MP4). Apify ran successfully but posted
nothing and returned no tweet ID.

Fix: post the auto-reply as a text tweet (caption only, no mediaUrl). This
guarantees the reply is posted. Added full response logging to getMovieUrl
so we can discover if Popcorn exposes an MP4 URL for native video later.

Also reset 12 stuck/failed video logs back to generating_video so they'll
be retried as text replies on the next cron cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace French technical terms with English equivalents

Technical terms like latency, traffic, errors, deployment, high availability,
static files, updates, response time, requests per second are now in English
while explanations remain in French, as per curriculum guidelines.

Security hardening: 6 fixes (C1, C2, C3, H1, H4, M3)

- C1: Sanitize console.error — safeErrorMessage() redacts sk-ant-* keys
- C2: Debug panel disabled in production (NODE_ENV gate)
- C3: Server-side player name validation (unicode regex, max 20 chars)

- H4: Human messages sandboxed in prompts to prevent injection
- M3: Security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options, nosniff, Referrer-Policy)

Weather (Python):

fix: flush remaining mailbox upserts after loop to prevent silent data loss

The batch flush condition (i === mailboxes.length - 1) was inside the loop
body after `continue` statements, so if the last mailbox(es) were skipped
(missing domain or capacity), all accumulated upserts were silently lost.

## Summary

- Bumps `swift-tools-version` to `6.2`

redundant)

`AuthorizationMiddleware`, single `database.url` env var (replaces
host/port/name/username/password)

`(databaseService:emailService:storageService:)`
- Adds full test suite: `AuthorizationTests`, `EmailServiceTests`,
`StorageServiceTests`, `TestJWT`

- Local server runs on port 8001

## Warning: Deployment Note

Production `main.swift` now reads a single `database.url` env var
instead of five separate vars (`database.host`, `database.port`,
`database.name`, `database.username`, `database.password`). Update
CodeBuild/ECS environment configuration before merging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix print footer with transform scaling approach (v2)

Previous percentage-based overrides didn't work because browsers
don't properly resolve percentage overrides on absolutely-positioned
elements with inline styles.

New approach: Keep pages at original 580x820px dimensions (matching
all inline pixel styles) and use transform: scale(1.3685) to
uniformly scale the entire page to fill A4 (210mm x 297mm).

Scale factor: 210mm / 153.47mm (580px) = 1.3685

This works because 580:820 has the same aspect ratio as A4's 210:297
(both ~0.707), so scaling is uniform with no distortion. All pixel
positioning stays correct since the page is still 580x820.

commit.message:
feat(arena): credible 5-gateway benchmark with agentgateway (CAB-1753) (#1593)

## Summary

Arena Bench v2 — credible 5-gateway benchmark with transparent
methodology.

- **agentgateway** (Linux Foundation / Solo.io): new K8s manifest +
GATEWAYS JSON config (6 features declared from research: llm_routing,
llm_cost, api_bridge, pii_detection, distributed_tracing, federation)
- **Gravitee**: features updated from [] to 5 dims reflecting 4.10
capabilities (api_bridge, llm_routing, llm_cost, guardrails,
distributed_tracing)
- **Kong Enterprise**: documented as not benchmarkable without license.
Kong OSS stays features=[]
- **METHODOLOGY.md**: scoring formulas, feature matrix, disclaimers,
reproducibility instructions
- **deploy.sh**: updated to 11 steps (added agentgateway deploy + health
check)

### Feature Matrix (L1)

| Gateway | MCP | Features Declared | Expected L1 Score |
|---------|-----|-------------------|-------------------|
| STOA | REST | 12/12 | ~85-95 |
| agentgateway | Streamable HTTP | 6/12 | ~40-55 |
| Gravitee | Streamable HTTP | 5/12 | ~35-50 |
| Kong OSS | None | 0/12 | ~5-8 |

### Council Validation

CAB-1753 — 8.25/10 (Go). 21 pts, 4 waves.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green (required checks)
- [ ] `kubectl apply -f k8s/arena/agentgateway.yaml` → pod Running
- [ ] agentgateway health returns 200
- [ ] L0 manual job includes agentgateway score > 0
- [ ] L1 manual job includes agentgateway enterprise_score > 0

commit.message:
fix: drawing tasks run to completion before checking target

For tasks with drawingPattern (e.g. challenge_d6 — two squares),
the character was stopping mid-execution when passing through the
target position during the inter-figure transition. This prevented
the second figure from being drawn at all.

Fix: skip early-exit on reachedTarget() when drawingPattern is set;
after full execution check final position instead of mid-run hit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add VibeStateChain + VibeStateVM — the virtual reality of consensus

The settlement layer is where Jarvis lives. Two contracts:

VibeStateChain — CKB-inspired state chain with Ergo subblocks:
- Header chain with prev_hash linkage (Nakamoto continuity)
- Regular blocks (12s heartbeat) + subblocks (2s fast finality)
- CKB cell model: typed state cells with create/consume/transform
- Consensus checkpointing from all VSOS consensus modules
- Validator management with stake + PoM weight
- Equivocation detection and slashing (Siren defense)
- Stateful layer separated from stateless consensus

VibeStateVM — RISC-V execution layer + NC-max + PoW locks:
- Matt's PoW lock script dependency: every cell consumption requires
  proof of work, solving the shared state UTXO contention problem
- Per-cell difficulty adjustment based on contention level
- Account/UTXO hybrid abstraction (CKB-VM architecture):
  Users see accounts, protocol uses UTXO for parallelism
- Siren commit-reveal for state transitions (anti-MEV)
- NC-max uncle blocks for fair difficulty adjustment
- RISC-V script registry for type/lock script verification
- Conceptually RISC-V ISA — EVM validates proofs of execution
